response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise from GISCollective and prior work, plus solid distributed-systems understanding from infrastructure projects. However, the primary job requirement—modern C++ development—is a significant gap: most substantial C++ work was 2014–2015. Could succeed if willing to rapidly ramp on systems-level C++ or if organization values domain expertise enough to invest in language upskilling.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise from GISCollective platform and Green Map work, directly applicable to routing-tile data
Proven understanding of distributed systems, low-latency infrastructure, and operational reliability from Dunnhumby (millions of RPS, async I/O, real-time metadata)
Demonstrated ability to learn and master new technical stacks across 16+ years (from web to embedded to systems), showing willingness to expand across full technical spectrum
concerns: C++ expertise is outdated (last significant commercial work 2014–2015); job requires modern C++ as primary skill
No recent systems programming, low-level optimization, or performance-critical work in current resume; recent roles focus on high-level languages (TypeScript, DLang)
No demonstrated experience building navigation engines, routing algorithms, or production navigation guidance systems

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain expertise and GIS background
